actors.name: Péter Magyar
actors.blurb: Péter Magyar is generating significant coverage as Hungary's incoming Prime Minister following the Tisza Party's landslide victory in the recent elections. His actions, including negotiations with the EU over frozen funds and allegations about oligarchs fleeing Hungary, have positioned him as a key figure in reshaping Hungary's political and economic landscape. Magyar's direct and often confrontational style has also drawn media attention.

actors.name: Giorgia Meloni
actors.blurb: Giorgia Meloni continues to be a prominent figure in right-wing news coverage due to her government's policies and international engagements. Recent controversies include her handling of Italy's energy crisis, tensions with Switzerland over medical costs for fire victims, and her stance on inviting Vladimir Putin to the G20 summit. Meloni's responses to these issues, along with her condemnation of political violence following the assassination attempt on Donald Trump, have kept her in the media spotlight.

actors.name: Viktor Orbán
actors.blurb: Viktor Orbán remains a significant figure in Hungarian politics despite his party's recent election defeat. His decision to step down from his parliamentary seat and focus on reorganizing Fidesz has drawn considerable attention. Orbán's legacy, including allegations of oligarchs fleeing Hungary with significant assets, continues to be a major topic of discussion in the media.
